title: ageratina rafaelensis
intro: Ageratina rafaelensis, also known as Rafael's ageratina or "Hierba del Palo", is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. Ageratina rafaelensis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
